description_long: A lush cluster of green, broad-leaved aquatic plants is shown, each with large, round, slightly cupped leaves that have prominent white veins radiating from the center. Rising above the leaves are several tall, slender stems, each topped with a delicate white flower that appears to be a calla lily or lotus-like bloom. The flowers have a trumpet-shaped structure with gently curled edges, and their creamy white color contrasts with the deep green of the foliage. The leaves are arranged in a somewhat overlapping, layered fashion, creating a dense, natural grouping. The stems are thin and flexible, supporting the flowers well above the leaf canopy. This plant grouping would be commonly found in urban parks, water gardens, or decorative landscaping near sidewalks and ponds.
description: A dense cluster of broad, round green leaves with tall stems bearing elegant white, trumpet-shaped flowers. The arrangement is lush and natural, ideal for urban parks or water features.
description_view_0: Front view shows the dense overlapping of large green leaves and several white flowers rising above on tall stems.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the varying heights of the stems and the cupped shape of the leaves, with some flowers partially hidden.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the back sides of the leaves and the arrangement of stems, with more flowers visible from behind.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizes the spread of the leaves and the upright posture of the tallest flowers.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, trumpet-like configuration, with each flower displaying a single, continuous, funnel-shaped petal that curls outward at the rim, typical of calla lilies or similar aquatic flowers.
